missing_info: Exact timestamps of the oper-state changes for each interface and the source of the alert (syslog line/snmp-trap/monitoring timestamp) to correlate events.
Full 'show interfaces Serial0/1/1' output from R2 and from R3 taken at (or immediately before/after) the alert time (includes line protocol, error counters, last input/last output, CRC/errors).
'show controllers serial 0/1/1' (or platform equivalent) from both R2 and R3 for the alert time window to check DTE/DCE, clocking, carrier, and physical-layer alarms.

Buffered syslog or logging output from both routers covering ±5 minutes around the alert timestamps (to reveal interface flaps, clocking errors, link/line protocol messages, or configuration changes).

Records of any recent configuration changes or maintenance actions (who/what/when) affecting Serial0/1/1 on R2 or R3, including CLI commands (shut/no shut), clocking changes, or remote provider notifications.

data.intent_description: R2 Serial0/1/1 moved to oper-state lower-layer-down while admin up; R3 Serial0/1/1 is administratively down (oper down). Goal: restore the serial link to operational up state.

plan.problem: R2 Serial0/1/1 oper=lower-layer-down, admin=up; R3 Serial0/1/1 oper=down, admin=down. The remote end (R3) is administratively down, preventing the link from coming up.
plan.fix_summary: Bring R3 Serial0/1/1 admin state to up (no shutdown) to restore the link. If the link does not return to up, perform an interface bounce on R2 Serial0/1/1 (shutdown then no shutdown). Risk: bouncing R2 interface will cause a brief outage on that link.

data.intent_description: R2 Serial0/1/1 moved to oper-state lower-layer-down while admin up; R3 Serial0/1/1 is administratively down (oper down). Goal: restore the serial link to operational up state.

evidence.detail: R2 Serial0/1/1 neighbors R3 at 10.0.0.6 (neighbor_id 3.3.3.3) and R3 Serial0/1/1 neighbors R2 at 10.0.0.5 (neighbor_id 2.2.2.2).
R2 Serial0/1/0 has OSPF neighbor at 10.0.0.1 (neighbor_id 1.1.1.1) — described in R2 ospf/running-config.

missing_info: Exact timestamps of the oper-state changes for each interface and the source of the alert (syslog line/snmp-trap/monitoring timestamp) to correlate events.
Full 'show interfaces Serial0/1/1' output from R2 and from R3 taken at (or immediately before/after) the alert time (includes line protocol, error counters, last input/last output, CRC/errors).
'show controllers serial 0/1/1' (or platform equivalent) from both R2 and R3 for the alert time window to check DTE/DCE, clocking, carrier, and physical-layer alarms.

Buffered syslog or logging output from both routers covering ±5 minutes around the alert timestamps (to reveal interface flaps, clocking errors, link/line protocol messages, or configuration changes).

Records of any recent configuration changes or maintenance actions (who/what/when) affecting Serial0/1/1 on R2 or R3, including CLI commands (shut/no shut), clocking changes, or remote provider notifications.

plan.problem: R2 Serial0/1/1 oper=lower-layer-down, admin=up; R3 Serial0/1/1 oper=down, admin=down. The remote end (R3) is administratively down, preventing the link from coming up.
plan.fix_summary: Bring R3 Serial0/1/1 admin state to up (no shutdown) to restore the link. If the link does not return to up, perform an interface bounce on R2 Serial0/1/1 (shutdown then no shutdown). Risk: bouncing R2 interface will cause a brief outage on that link.

evidence: get_interface_status(router2:Serial0/1/1) returned admin_state: up, oper_state: up, last_change: 2026-05-27T14:55:52.000117+00:00 (tool_call_id: call_w49fARNXLW9d0cmWT4nhDiiI).
get_interface_status(router3:Serial0/1/1) returned admin_state: up, oper_state: up, last_change: 2026-05-27T14:13:53.000756+00:00 (tool_call_id: call_dGwiRljrURC8bPGwtsHCpggm).
get_ospf(router2) shows Serial0/1/1 with neighbor 3.3.3.3 (10.0.0.6) in state ospf-nbr-full (tool_call_id: call_wEkAZFQl4nAm1oVIKik8ioKR).
get_ospf(router3) shows Serial0/1/1 with neighbor 2.2.2.2 (10.0.0.5) in state ospf-nbr-full (tool_call_id: call_rtH0bGV6b2hSrphN9yfrD3AV).

messages.content:
What was observed
- R2 Serial0/1/1: admin up, oper = lower-layer-down (link not passing traffic).
- R3 Serial0/1/1: reported administratively down in the alert/context (remote end preventing link).

Diagnosis (main cause)
- The remote interface was administratively down / the remote-end state prevented the line protocol from coming up (or the local interface was stuck awaiting carrier). A remote admin-down or transient physical/clocking issue prevented the serial link from negotiating.

Changes made
- Attempted to enable R3 Serial0/1/1 (no-op: already enabled).
- Bounced R2 Serial0/1/1 (shutdown then no shutdown).

Verification
- Passed. Post-change checks show:
  - R2 Serial0/1/1: admin up, oper up (last_change recorded).
  - R3 Serial0/1/1: admin up, oper up (last_change recorded).
  - OSPF on both routers: neighbor relationship on the link is FULL (neighbors 3.3.3.3 and 2.2.2.2).
- Remaining issues: none.

- R2 Serial0/1/1: admin up, oper = lower-layer-down (link not passing traffic).
- R3 Serial0/1/1: reported administratively down in the alert/context (remote end preventing link).

Diagnosis (main cause)
- The remote interface was administratively down / the remote-end state prevented the line protocol from coming up (or the local interface was stuck awaiting carrier). A remote admin-down or transient physical/clocking issue prevented the serial link from negotiating.

- Bounced R2 Serial0/1/1 (shutdown then no shutdown).
